name: AWS Well-Architected Framework
description: AWS Well-Architected Framework — cloud architecture best practices organized across six pillars: Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, and Sustainability. Security Pillar is the primary security-relevant section.
relationToCloudSecurity: Foundational AWS-specific guidance. Customers running on AWS commonly use Well-Architected Reviews as part of design and audit cycles.
relationToAISecurity: AI workloads on AWS (SageMaker, Bedrock) inherit Well-Architected guidance; AWS published an AI/ML lens extension.

license.notes: Vendor-published framework. Verify specific license terms at the source URL before redistribution.

current_extraction.notes: Source PDF from docs.aws.amazon.com (~200 pages), marker-extracted to markdown. Per-best-practice structured CSV/JSON generated by extract_aws_wa.py (63 of 64 SECnn-BPnn best practices across 11 questions). Question-level headings were flattened by marker; we parse at the BP level which is the natural unit of assessment.

notes: Metadata stub; acquire source and extract structured form as follow-up.
